entry.title: Re: Have SAP HANA Database Explorer Extension for VSCode...

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content: R&D confirmed both x86 and x64 exporting to ODBC has an issue so that's what they will be fixing. Go here for the update when it's available: https://origin.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/site/index.html

entry.title: Re: Interchangeability groups in MRP Area
entry.content:
Hi,  so far i got now reply.  I checked the S/4 System and i found coding from SAP which make it system wise impossible to create Interchangeability groups for MRP Area without modification.

Coding you can find in the

Programm /SAPAPO/SAPLRRP_PACK_MAINTAIN

 Form UPDATE_MARC_CHANGES_IN_DB Line 340 until 351

entry.title: Re: How to redeem a campaign code to get a free SAP Certification exam attempt and schedule your exa
entry.content:
Hello David,

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or undeserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
    • First generation college students
    • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I am willing to learn more about SAP and to undergo the rigors of studying for SAP Certification. I have completed my first learning journey Project Manage SAP S/4 Hana Implementation and I awaiting my certification attempt

Thank you

Ahmed

entry.title: Re: How to Use Custom Fields for Pricing in Sales Documents
entry.content:
Hi @Mertcan,
Currently there is no option to update prices automatically. What is your exact requirement? In which cases to you want to update prices automatically?

There is already a similar feature request in SAP Customer Influence where you can vote for to give it more priority in planning.
https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/292742

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: how to download application log message through custom program?
entry.content: Application Log – Guidelines for Developers (BC-SRV-BAL)

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Wien 07.11.2024
entry.content: BTW: ich werde auch später kommen. haben jour fixe und ich nutze die Gelegenheit für #sitVIE-promotion.

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi Olga 

Our S4 system has been copied into a sandbox and the sum process has been run, and the Deletion transports where not included or generated. (we are using the CCM on BTP)

Can we still generate these maybe somewhere else and the import them after the SUM process ??

The proper should have been. (broadly only regardign to CCM and SUM)

1. Create a copy of the ECC system to upgrade in the Sandbox

2. Run the analysis of CCM and scoping on the sandbox based on the ASUG data,

3  generate the Deletion transport

4, Run the SUM process cand include the transport in the SUM  process to convert the system.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Applications Studio 2411 (Pilot) can not do...
entry.content: Hi Anant, we are facing the same issue. could we ask what do you mean by backend chagnes and how we can correct those? Thanks!

entry.title: Unable to create text with text rich editor
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

The option of text rich edition for text fields is disable can anyone tell me where is the option to enable it?

entry.title: Re: BFC: Trigger condition in formula based on RU?
entry.content:
Hello, can I have two trigger conditions? Here is an example of the formula and the two trigger conditions I would like to add:

Formula: {AC in (FAMREF=BL1-F0002) ; FL in (F20; F30; F40; F50; F60; F61; F62; F70)} = {DP = MONTH(-1; REF_PER) ; PE = MONTH(-1; REF_PER)}

Trigger conditions: [DP > DFIRST] AND [RU in (ETYPE=S)]

For your information, I tried to include the second condition ([RU in (ETYPE=S)]) in the formula, but it didn't work.

Thank you for helping me solve the issue

entry.title: Re: How do I read an FTP server file from a BTP ABAP envi...
entry.content:
I hope you're talking about sFTP.

I would think you have to use SAP Cloud Integration to achieve this.

entry.title: Maintenance order printing getting time out
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are printing the maintenance order from edit mode and from additional functions to print.

If the number of operations in the maintenance order is more (say 75 in our case) if we use the print queue and sent an output, it is throwing an error "500 internal server error".

So, instead of using the print queue we click on print preview so that the pdf will display and then printing it manually, but while doing it loading of PDF is taking the more time.

Could you please let us know the resolution/reason for the inconsistency. 

Regards,

Yaswanth

 @Ravisha 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content: Wow, that's insane that it took more than 4 years for this to be addressed. Well, now I can honestly say I was involved in making Crystal Reports better....for what it's worth. 😄

entry.title: Re: How to configured XBRL options in SAP DM?
entry.content: Hi Marc, I would also need some documentation. Can I send you an email?

entry.title: Re: Consumption of an API within a Custom POD Plugin in S...
entry.content: In the plugin controller you can create an URL like this example for the get work center public API: var url = this.getPublicApiRestDataSourceUri() + "workcenter/v1/workcenters?plant=" + plant + "&workCenter=" + workCenter; Then you can use this.ajaxGetRequest wo make the API call for this URL

entry.title: Re: Still unable to pass DateTime parameters with "MS OLE...
entry.content:
What version of MS SQL Server are you using?

According to the CR Platforms info ODBC 19 is only supported in MS SQL 2022:

 

2019ODBC BI 4.3 SP01+Microsoft ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server
OLEDB BI 4.3 SP01+Microsoft OLEDB Driver 18 for SQL Server
JDBCBI 4.3 SP01+Microsoft JDBC Driver 8.2 for SQL Server
ODBCBI 4.3 SP01+DataDirect ODBC (10)
2022ODBC BI 4.3 SP04+Microsoft ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server
OLEDB BI 4.3 SP04+Microsoft OLEDB Driver 19 for SQL Server
JDBCBI 4.3 SP04+Microsoft JDBC Driver 12.2 for SQL Server
ODBCBI 4.3 SP04+DataDirect ODBC (10)

So what CR does is when the DB Client dll cannot be found what it does is default to a generic SQL Syntax, likely cause why the Syntax for the Parameter is different.

The other option is test using an ODBC connection, just as a test, to see if ODBC generates the correct syntax.

Note the patch level is the same for CR 2020 Designer as noted for BI 4.3

entry.title: Re: How do I read an FTP server file from a BTP ABAP envi...
entry.content: Could you please share some information on FTP Cloud integration for blogs, specifically for reading files? It could be really helpful for me.

entry.title: Re: ERR! CRWEB00000119
entry.content:
Likely cause is you don't have the database client installed or configured to work on the CRS Server.

Or you don't have permissions to access the DB, CR fully supports Database Security.

Check with your CRS Administrator to see if they get the same error and if you have permissions to view that report.

entry.title: Re: Upload Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable...
entry.content: Hi team, the problem is that import supplier invoices or upload customer open items apps are only to upload invoices or credit memo. It is not possible to post other type of posting. Is there another solution?

entry.title: Re: Crystal reports 2020 export to odbc
entry.content: Actually 4 years is misleading. Without doing a bunch of testing it's possible this was only an issue since some 2016 patch may have broke it....

entry.title: Re: Forbidden AI Launchpad
entry.content: Same here...

entry.title: Re: TSL field value in ACDOCU is not the same as in ACDOCA
entry.content:
change the breakdown category per note 3144691  and/or import the notes 3204008 , 3302620 

note 3144691 - Group Reporting: Data release of transaction currency amount
note 3204008 - Data Release Task: Inaccurate transaction currency and transaction currency amount
note 3302620 - RUJ: Evaluate Breakdown Category for Transaction Currency

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA PPDS con IBP SUPPLY, REQUERIMIENTOS HEURISTIC SAP_PP_002
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are working whit  SAP PPDS that is embedded with SAP S/4 HANA version 2023.

We run the heuristic SAP_PP_002 to generate the planned orders.

This heuristic considers the following requirements:

1) Sales forecast

2) Sales orders

3) Safety stock

4) Stock transport requests

The SAP S/4 HANA PPDS is coexisting with the IBP Supply, and this generates the KEY FIGURE TARGET.

In some runs we are going to take the above 4 requirements, but in other runs we will only take the KEY FIGURE TARGET.

I have the following questions:

1) How can I get the heuristic SAP_PP_002 to not consider the 4 requirements above and only consider the KEY FIGURE TARGET?

2) Or what other heuristic can I use to take only the KET FIGURE TARGET as a requirement?

3) Is there any BADI, BAPI or ENHANCEMENT that can help me?

Best regards,

Arnulfo Cordero Pradel

-people looking to switch their careers mid life

Regards

entry.title: How to trigger a workflow when doing a Temporary Change on a Work Schedule?
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I found this ( Workflows for Temporary Changes | SAP Help Portal )information by SAP that explains how to trigger an approval workflow when creating a Temporary Change on the employee's Work Schedule through the Administer Time menu. I followed the information and testes several scenarios but did not manage to trigger the workflow. 

 

Did you ever did a configuration like this before? What could possibly be the error that I am doing?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Is it possible to change sales orders through spreadsheets?
entry.content:
Hello,

we have the requirement to mass change sales orders and add new schedule line items to existing sales orders through spreadsheets. Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to change sales orders through spreads...
entry.content:
Hello,

Currently, in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it's not possible to change existing sales orders through spreadsheets, you can only create new ones.
Please check the official documentation here: Import Sales Orders | SAP Help Portal

However, you can accomplish your requirement through Sales APIs. It's possible to mass change sales orders and add new items to existing sales orders through the following APIs:

Sales Order (A2X, OData V2) | SAP Help Portal
Operations for Sales Order (A2X, OData V2) | SAP Help Portal

Sales Order (A2X, OData V4) | SAP Help Portal
Operations for Sales Order (A2X, OData V4) | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @S0022323026 ,

if you haven't created the deletion transport on the ECC system before conversion you can create a  new custom code analysis project in the CCM ap on SAP BTP to analyze the code in your converted S4 sandbox, upload the collected usage data and create deletion transport on the S/4 sandbox. We recommend to backup then the unused code, see also the blog How to backup custom code with gCTS.

Kind regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Elimination postings in group reporting
entry.content: My understanding is that you want to see all eliminations on PL 20 and PL30, then you need to specify Group value in the prompt and only bring the consolidation unit dimension rather then consolidation unit eliminated dimension as well as posting level into the layout?

entry.title: Re: Keep the original text in balance carry forward items instead on "BCF" in Group Report
entry.content: TEXT in ACDOCU is derived from the name of each GR automation task.

@Rene_de_Daniel , A couple of questions,

What is the future of SAP Enterprise Architecture Designer ?

Is there any path to migrate from EAD to LeanIX?

Regards,

Brian

entry.title: Join Us in Shaping the Future with the SAP Business Technology Platform!
entry.content:
At SAP, we believe that collaboration is key to driving innovation. We’re excited to invite our valued customers and partners to engage in a series of groundbreaking projects leveraging the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). This powerful platform integrates data management, analytics, application development, and intelligent technologies, enabling you to unlock new possibilities for your business.

Why Get Involved?

Participating in these projects allows you to harness the full potential of the SAP BTP. With its low-code development capabilities, cloud security features, and AI-driven solutions, the platform empowers you to streamline operations and enhance decision-making. Your insights and expertise are crucial in shaping tools that align with your unique needs and challenges.

Explore Our Upcoming Projects

We’re focusing on a variety of innovative initiatives built on the capabilities of SAP BTP, including:

  • Low-Code Development: Simplify application creation and process automation to enhance agility.
  • Cloud Security Enhancements: Strengthen your security posture with advanced encryption and key management solutions.
  • AI-Powered Insights: Leverage AI to transform unstructured data into actionable insights.
  • Automation and Integration: Discover how to automate processes seamlessly across your organization.

How to Participate

If you’re interested in getting involved, we’d love to hear from you! Reach out to us to express your interest in participating in these exciting initiatives. Together, we can leverage the SAP Business Technology Platform to drive innovation and create impactful solutions that help your organization thrive.

Thank you for being part of our journey. We look forward to collaborating with you to shape the future of technology!

Register until November 15, 2024 and take advantage of this opportunity!

SAP Business Technology Platform projects for registration:

 

  • GenAI Content Provenance and Authenticity via Watermarking
  • Investigation into a Customer Security Dashboard
  • AI-Powered Autonomous Sandbox
  • Discover SAP Build Process Automation Triggers: Empowering Experts to Automate and Extend Use Cases
  • Cloud Security: Improving the Encryption Key Management Experience
  • Knowledge Graph RAG on unstructured Data
  • AI Agent Builder - A Low-Code Innovation with SAP Build

 

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com to register.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

If you are also interested in other CEI projects that are currently open for registration, see here the full list.

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Company code numbering release 2408
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

I would like to know if, in release 2408, the logic for numbering the company code has changed.

In CBC, when I define the organisation structure and thus also the company code, is it possible to use a letter as the initial character in the four digits, or must I use a number as the first character? E.g.: "IT10".

Thank you

Francesco

entry.title: Re: Request Help to Learn SuccessFactors
entry.content: @salimo, as you go through the courses, the product documentation should assist you with the journey: SAP SuccessFactors HCM.

entry.title: How can a VBA script "read" the contents of a SAP table?
entry.content:
Hello, everyone. I hope you all are having a lovely day.

I have a VBA script that extracts some data from SAP PM. It goes into IW29, chooses a specific variant and loads the notifications. Then, it selects all of them to show the relevant orders. After loading the orders, it needs to select a specific display variant before extracting the information to an Excel file. The way this is done is as follows:

When selecting the menu option to select a display variant, SAP opens a table. Each row is a different variable. The script has, defined within the code, the index of the variant I'm interested in and selects the corresponding row.

The problem is when the index for the variant changes for some reason (i.e. a new variant before it was created. SAP sorts the variants in the table in alphabetical order). When this happens, I have to manually look for the new index and change the value in the code.

Is there a way to make the script autohandle this? I thought about reading the 1st column of every row and, when the content matches a predefined string within the code, the script selects that row, but I have no idea on how to do this...

I'll share the relevant part of the code below. A couple notes about it:

  • The SAP I use is in Portuguese, so my bad if I mistranslated something
  • For those that don't know, the ' symbol is what's used to create comments in VBS/VBA. I also highlighted the comments in green, just like in VBA

CODE:
===============================================================================

' IW39 EXTRACTION
' ACCESSING IW29
  session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").Text = "/nIW29"
  session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0

' LOOKING FOR NOTIFICATION VARIANT
  session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[17]").press ' Button "Call variant"
  session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/txtV-LOW").Text = "<insert any variant here>" ' Selecting the notifications variant
  session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/txtENAME-LOW").Text = "" ' Clears the field "Created by"
  session.findById("wnd[1]/tbar[0]/btn[8]").press ' Press F8

' RUNNING IW29
  session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[8]").press ' Press F8

' ACCESSING THE ORDERS FROM THE NOTIFICATIONS
  session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/cntlGRID1/shellcont/shell").setCurrentCell -1, ""
  session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/cntlGRID1/shellcont/shell").SelectAll ' Button on the top left that selects all notifications
  session.findById("wnd[0]/mbar/menu[4]/menu[0]").Select ' Environment ---> Orders

' SELECTING THE DISPLAY VARIANT (the problem lies here)
  session.findById("wnd[0]/mbar/menu[5]/menu[2]/menu[1]").Select ' Configurations ---> Display Variants ---> Call...  
 session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/ssubD0500_SUBSCREEN:SAPLSLVC_DIALOG:0501/cntlG51_CONTAINER/shellcont/shell").setCurrentCell IndexIW39, "TEXT" ' <insert any variant here>. The variable "IndexIW39" is a number defined elsewhere. It represents the row of the desired variant in the table that's on the screen at this point 
 session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/ssubD0500_SUBSCREEN:SAPLSLVC_DIALOG:0501/cntlG51_CONTAINER/shellcont/shell").selectedRows = CStr(IndiceIW39) ' CStr converts the "IndexIW39" value to a string 
 session.findById("wnd[1]/usr/ssubD0500_SUBSCREEN:SAPLSLVC_DIALOG:0501/cntlG51_CONTAINER/shellcont/shell").clickCurrentCell

' code continues after this point...

===============================================================================

 IMPORTANT: If you'd like to run this script yourself, the next bit of code is mandatory BEFORE the previous code, as it is this second piece of code that allows VBS/VBA to interact with SAP:
===============================================================================

' CONNECTING WITH SAP
  If Not IsObject(app) Then
    Set SapGuiAuto = GetObject("SAPGUI")
    Set app = SapGuiAuto.GetScriptingEngine
  End If

  If Not IsObject(Connection) Then
    Set Connection = app.Children(0)
  End If

  If Not IsObject(session) Then
    Set session = Connection.Children(0)
  End If

  If IsObject(WScript) Then
    WScript.ConnectObject session, "on"
    WScript.ConnectObject app, "on"
  End If

  session.findById("wnd[0]").maximize

===============================================================================

You can use a .xlsm or .vbs file to run the script.
Pro tip: in an Excel file (.xlsm) you can run the script step by step pressing F8 in the Visual Script window. You can also add breakpoints.

entry.title: Re: SPC found An internal error occurred;
entry.content:
Try updating the saved MESYS password: see 2409116 - Internal Error displaying SPC Chart - SAP for Me

Regards,

Andrew.

entry.title: Re: How to find package S4HANA2023CORE HANA DB Export 1
entry.content: I had the same problem and found the packages in:

entry.title: Re: How to find package S4HANA2023CORE HANA DB Export 1
entry.content:
I had the same problem and found the package in:
INSTALLATIONS & UPGRADES → By Categary → SAP Application Components → SAP S/4HANA → SAP S/4HANA 2023 → INSTALLATION (AUTOM. DOWNLOAD)

This folder contains S4CORE108_INST_EXPORT_<n>.zip, where <n> ranges from 1 to 29. The first screen in SWPM 2.0 asked for *1.zip, but the next screen asks for 2 to 29. Downloading all of these manually is a pain, so consider adding them to your download basket and using the SAP Download Manager.

entry.title: SAP Master Data Governance: A Leader Among Users
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) has consistently been rated as a Leader in the Fall 2024 G2 Grid for Master Data Management (MDM) on the G2 peer to peer review site, and the reasons are clear.

First and foremost, SAP MDG offers a comprehensive and intuitive platform for managing multi-domain master data across the enterprise. With its user-friendly interface and robust capabilities, organizations can easily create, update, and maintain their business-critical master data in a centralized and controlled environment, while leveraging 3rd data quality and enrichment services to automate the process.

One of the key features that sets SAP MDG apart is its ability to streamline data governance processes and ensure data quality and consistency. The platform offers a wide range of data governance capabilities, including data validation, standardization, correction, enrichment, and consolidation, which helps organizations maintain high-quality and accurate master data thru a single lens.  The solution also provides data quality management, enabling organizations to define master data quality rules, striving for consistent and validated data across all points of entry while also enabling data quality monitoring and remediation.

Another key feature of SAP MDG is its tight integration with other SAP solutions, which allows organizations to leverage existing data models, business logic, and validation frameworks. This integration not only streamlines data management processes but also enhances collaboration between different departments by providing a unified view of master data. Furthermore, the application supports open integration with third-party products and services, ensuring that organizations can maintain a flexible and adaptable technology stack. Its combination of centralized governance with decentralized ownership through federation, along with seamless integration capabilities, positions it as a valuable asset for any enterprise aiming to optimize its data landscape and drive better business outcomes.

Lastly, the robust support and training resources offered by SAP further contribute to the platform's high user satisfaction ratings. Users appreciate the strong customer support and extensive training materials that are available to help them maximize the value of SAP MDG within their organizations.

Overall, SAP Master Data Governance's ability to centralize and streamline master data management processes, its seamless integration with other SAP applications, and its strong support and training resources all contribute to its high user satisfaction. As more organizations recognize the importance of effective master data management, it's no surprise that SAP MDG continues to be a leader in the master data management market space among users.

Learn more:

  • SAP Master Data Governance on G2
  • SAP Community for SAP Master Data Governance

entry.title: Re: Adding specific image for each item
entry.content: @lilymar, you could try the "Image" or the "Image URL" options in the item record > "Item Details" tab. If you don't see the fields, review the following document: Displaying Fields in an Entity. Please, before trying to configure the "Image URL" field, review the KBA 2979432.

entry.title: Re: CFOP Wrong determination MM
entry.content: oi Gabriela, nao, tudo e estandar, configuracao cfop em spro

entry.title: Issue with performance management
entry.content:
Hi, we are running into some issues when using calibration sessions for year end review in performance management

Here are a couple of areas that Success Factors could improve:

    • Issue is that when there’s a legal entity transfer, the performance form has to be recreated then loaded under the new ID.  In order for the form to show up in the session, we have to deactivate the session, add the new employee, and then reactivate session.  When this happens, if we have moved any forms from one status to another Self-Review to Manager Review (to lock the ratings), said forms will be removed from the session.  This is not ideal as managers may want to use the Dashboard tab of the session to view rating distribution and may become confused when their employees’ rows disappear.
      • is there a transfer a users information in a calibration session? or a way to load directly to a specific status? 
  • To enhance change management, it would be convenient for their employees’ forms to still show up for managers under Performance and Assessment, in addition to showing up in the session.
    • We’re able to make the forms appear there by turning on “Out of Turn” access, but those forms don’t show the employees’ input because the system sees them as draft forms.  This means that if managers were to edit those forms using Out of Turn access, the inputs would not appear in the forms showing up in the session – nor would the content they add from the session view show up in the forms displayed in the Performance and Assessment view.

entry.title: Re: Request for Guidance on Variable Pay setup integrated...
entry.content:
Hi @satyajit_2719 

 

1/ It should be doable in standard this way (screenshot below), otherwise only define mapping to PayComp group and use employee history export/import feature (compatible with EC employee history processor) to load the earnings specifically for bi-weekly salary employees. You need to understand whether the "earnings" they want to load are already impacted by proration or not. See blog 1 and blog 2. 

 

2/ Either add it to the main record eligibility rule that controls which records are retrieved. Or if they have Time Off driving changes in EC, then setup the workaround proposed here.

 

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Total Compensation History Widget - Display Logic
entry.content:
Hi @lsnakkers 

Confirmed not possible in the standard EC chart, you could do a similar one that would meet the requirement through story reports however and share the link in EC portlet. 

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Error SAP_IPA_28014: Unable to process the requested Document Information Extraction
Creating a Dashboard from Address Cleansing Results using SAP Data Quality Management Microservices
entry.content:
In my last blog post, I introduced our GitHub sample for working with SAP Datasphere and SAP Data Quality Management, microservices. Today we are announcing our new sample to show how you might create a data quality dashboard from your address cleansing and geocoding results. If you are not familiar with our GitHub sample structure, please refer to the previous article first.

 

Dashboard sample using the result from SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data

Our sample can be found in the SAP-samples / cloud-dqm-sample-payloads repository.

/ datasphere-dashboard

This sample uses the additional details generated during the address cleansing and geocoding process to create a statistical visualization using SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

Data Quality Dashboard

In SAP Analytics Cloud, you can create a dashboard to visualize the total number of records for various informational codes received from SAP Data Quality Management microservices for location data. In our example, we used the pie and bar chart representations as shown below.

 

Output Field Properties

SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data generates several Informational codes that can be used to create reports. These codes indicate how well the address data was matched to the reference data and provide additional information if the best possible results are not achieved. To generate these codes, you must include the informational codes as output field properties in each request to the service.

The sample dashboard above was created using some of these output fields, but you can create your own reports using any output field properties as described in the documentation: Assignment Codes and Identify an Invalid Address.

 

Address Validation Summary

The output field addr_asmt_info provides the information about the validity of the address. If it is V (Valid) or C (Corrected), it is considered that the address returned is validated to the full depth in the reference data. If it is I (Invalid) or B (Blank), you may be able to remedy these addresses by enabling the Suggestion Lists properties.

Valid

V (Valid)

Address was validated based on the depth of reference data and received no changes or only minor changes

C (Corrected)

Address was corrected during the validation process and was validated based on the depth of reference data

Invalid

I (Invalid)

Address did not not match to the full depth of reference data

B (Blank)

The request had blank values in all address input field properties

 

Address Validation Level

The output field addr_asmt_level provides the level that the address matches to the address reference data.

Premise

S (Secondary)

Address was validated to the secondary address information such as a floor and unit number

PR (Primary Range)

Address was validated to the house number

Street

PN (Street)

Address was validated to the street

City

L4 (Locality4)

Address was validated to the fourth level of city

L3 (Locality3)

Address was validated to the third level of city

L2 (Locality4)

Address was validated to the second level of city

L1 (Locality1)

Address was validated to the first level of city

Region

R (Region)

Address was validated to the region

C (Country)

Address was validated to the country

Unassigned

X (Unassigned)

Address was not validated to any level of data

 

Geocode Validation Level

The output field geo_asmt_level provides the level that the address matches to the geocode reference data.

Premise

PRE (Primary Range Exact)

Address was geocoded to the exact house location

PRI (Primary Range Interpolated)

Address was geocoded to the interpolated location based on the house number range

Street

PN (Primary Name)

Address was geocoded to the midpoint of a street segment

Postcode

PF (Postcode Full)

Address was geocoded to the center of a full postcode area

P2P (Postcode2 Partial)

Address was geocoded to the center of a partial postcode2 area

P1 (Postcode1)

Address was geocoded to the center of a postcode1 area

City

L3 (Locality3)

Address was geocoded to the center of a third-level city area

L2 (Locality2)

Address was geocoded to the center of a second-level city area

L1 (Locality1)

Address was geocoded to the center of a first-level city area

Unassigned

X (Unassigned)

Address was not geocoded to any level of data

 

Reasons for Invalid Addresses

The output fields addr_info_code and addr_info_code_msg provide the potential reasons why an address was not fully validated to the full depth in the reference data.  You can find the list of codes and associated messages in the documentation: Information Codes for Address Cleanse.

Information Code

1000’s

Potential problems related to the input country or script

2000’s

Potential problems related to the input city, region, or postcode

3000’s

Potential problems related to the input street, building, house number, or postbox number

4000’s

Potential problems related to the input secondary address information or organization name

5000’s

Miscellaneous scenarios encountered when matching the input address to reference data. Not all of these scenarios are necessarily problems

 

We Value Your Feedback!

In this blog post, we showed an example of interpreting the output data generated by SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data, creating the data model in SAP Datasphere, and visualizing the data to create a data quality dashboard.

If you like what we did in our sample, we would appreciate your feedback on it. Is there any additional information you would like to get from the service? Do you want to see similar reports from the Data Quality Service UI? If you would like to send us an enhancement request, please use the following channel to contact us.

  • Enhancement requests to SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data
  • Enhancement requests to SAP Datasphere
  • Enhancement requests to SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Enhancement requests to SAP S/4HANA Cloud for MDM

entry.title: Re: Adding node id in process.xml
entry.content: Has anyone successfully used this? I tried the <action id='actionId' bean='actionBean' node='#{configurationService.configuration.getProperty('custom.node.value')}' > suggestion but continuously get an error when running the process that says "'#{configurationService.configuration.getProperty('custom.node.value')}' is not a valid value for 'integer'." I do have custom.node.value=1 in my local.properties file. I have also tried with 'getInt' and 'getInteger'

entry.title: Re: Highlight Critical materials in IH01
entry.content: I used an implicit enhancement option at the end of form object_type_color  to add the needed code.

entry.title: AL requires that exempt overtime wages be reported in Box 14 W2.
entry.content:
The Alabama DOR website says:

ALDOR proposes that exempt overtime wages be reported in Box 14 of employees Form W-2 using “EX OT WAGES” as the indicator: for example, EX OT WAGES- $15,250

Is SAP creating a tax form group for this that can be added to box 14 of the W-2 for reporting?

entry.title: Important Tables for Header and Item Data in SAP
entry.content:
Following are the important tables for header and item data in SAP.

Table Description
WB2_MAP_CONTROLGT: Generation Status for Field
WB2_MAP_FIELDSTrading Contract: Mapping of Fi
WB2_V_EKKO_EKPOData Selection from PO
WB2_V_EKKO_EKPO2Data Selection from PO (without
WB2_V_LIKP_LIPSData Selection from Deliveries
WB2_V_LIKP_LIPS2Data Selection from Deliveries
WB2_V_MKPF_MSEGData Selection from Material Do
WB2_V_MKPF_MSEG2Data Selection from Material Do
WB2_V_RBKP_RSEGSelect Invoice Verification Doc
WB2_V_RBKP_RSEG2Select Invoice Verification Doc
WB2_V_VBAK_VBAPData Selection from SO
WB2_V_VBAK_VBAP2Data Selection from SO (without
WB2_V_VBRK_VBRLData Selection from Customer Bi
WB2_V_VBRK_VBRL2Data Selection from Cust. Billi
WB2_V_VBRK_VBRPData Selection from Customer Bi
WB2_V_VBRK_VBRP2Data Selection from Customer Bi
WB2_V_VBRK_WBGTGT: Database View for Different
WB2_V_WBHK_WBHIData Selection from TC
WB2_V_WBHK_WBHI2Data Selection from TC (without
WB2_V_WBRK_WBRLData Selection from Agency Docu
WB2_V_WBRK_WBRL2Data Selection from Agency Docu
WB2_V_WBRK_WBRPData Selection from Agency Docu
WB2_V_WBRK_WBRP2Data Selection from Agency Docu
WB2_V_WBRK_WBRP2             Data Selection from Agency Docu

entry.title: Re: How to add a custom generic card tile with micro comparison chart on Overview Page in VS Code
entry.content: Hi - any guidance anyone?

entry.title: Re: MESSAGE Unable to resolve plug-in "com.sap.adt.ddic.t...
entry.content: I will try to uninstall Eclipse and install it again. To me it looks like the last upgrade of the ADT Tools has a bug, Can anyone recommend another place to ask the people in charge of ADT?

entry.title: REMADV IDOC for Clearing Customer Credit Memo
entry.content:
Team, 

I have configured the REMADV IDOC to clear customer invoices and customer credit memos, but I am unable to clear the credit memo documents. I’m receiving a message indicating that the difference is too large. I'm not sure if REMADV has the capability to handle such scenarios. Could someone provide guidance on this?

FIN (Finance) FIN Accounts Receivable and Payable

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Wien 07.11.2024
entry.content: Die Diskussion über Konsequenzen wird schwierig, da es dabei dann auch um das verbotene Thema Fussball geht, und Du damit dann für Dich selbst auch Konsequenzen verhängen musst.....läuft auf ein perpetuum mobile hinaus...

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Product compliance - Safety Data Sheet creat...
entry.content:
Hello

Regarding your sentence:

" PC solution if classic will eventually be phased out."

the SAP Roadmap is clear. EHS Classic will be shut down and not supported in future S/4 HANA releases. There is no "eventually" in the Roadmap.

C.B.

entry.title: what to do if a success factors learning video times out
entry.content:
Hello,

A user of the Success Factors learning management system (LMS) has been assigned training in this regional platform. Whenever he needs to complete viewing a training video, the system times out after about half an hour. He uses Windows to sign in and uses a laptop computer as opposed to a Mac. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Payroll Third-Party Remittance(3PR) Evaluation Run in...
entry.content:
The error "Payroll area 04 is not released for payroll. Error during initialization of payroll" happens as the system is checking the Payroll Area status in PA03 during live run.
This check happens in include RPCHRT09_SET_PCR_CHECK_LOGIC:

*   New logic
    IF iv_sc_pa03_off EQ abap_true.
      cv_sw_check_pa03 = abap_false.
    ELSE.
      cv_sw_check_pa03 = abap_true.
    ENDIF.

  ELSE.

As from the migration to S/4, the K500 schema has a condition to read or not PA03 data.
In non S/4 systems, this check is commented in schema level, but that's not the case for S/4 systems, hence it reads PA03 payroll status and does not allow the run with the exit status.

You can check more details on that on KBA 0003498158 (Payroll Driver is ignoring PA03 status).

In any case, I see that you are performing the 3PR Evaluation by running the payroll with schema K500.
Please keep in mind that this process is very old and no longer supported.

Refer to note 0003495130 (3PR: Process models update for Canada): "The use of K500 schema is no longer supported for any process in the S/4HANA landscape."

Therefore, you should migrate from the Payroll + Schema K500 combination to the 3PR Evaluation program RPURME00.
Report RPURME00 is the standard and supported way to perform the 3PR evaluation on your system.

To do the switch from the old method (K500) to the new method (RPURME00), you'll have to perform the customization of P3PR/3PRTE and P3PR/TE_CA on table T77S0. Refer to KBA 0003132477 as an example.

entry.title: BYD2411 Request for standard XSL format for ISO20022 payment file
entry.content:
Dear sir/madam,

We want to do a minor transformation on the ISO20022 payment file. Is there a standard XSL template available for how the file is currently generated by SAP in the ISO20022 standard, which we can use to make our adaptations?

Thanks in advance.

Kind regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2411 Request for standard XSL format for ISO20022...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

To attend such customization requirement a complete step by step document is available in the Help Document below:

Generation of Custom Payment File Using XSL Transformation Repository
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/dcd38bb1a815492285215b240bf75062.html

A guide on how to deploy an XSLT transformation is attached to the KBA below (refer to "XSLT_PAYMENT_FLEXIBILITY.docx"):

3455390 - ISO20022 pain.001.001.09 XML-based Message Format Is Missing for Netherlands https://me.sap.com/notes/3455390

 

Best regards,  
Ihan
SAP Product Support
Community:
SAP Business ByDesign:
https://www.sap.com/community/topics/business-bydesign.html

entry.title: Re: Databases not show in choose company list in SAP B1 10.0 PL2405 HF1
entry.content: This looks like the SQL version, using port 30000 for the license server. Please check if you have the ODBC driver installed: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/ms-sql-2019-sap-10-pl02-b1-client/qaq-p/12268484

entry.title: S4HANA- MMBE display: How to hide the "Receiving valuated Stock in Tr"
entry.content: In S4 by default, when I run MMBE with the "No Zero Stock Lines" unchecked, the "Receiving valuated Stock in Tr" is displayed in the hierarchy display row. Please advise on how to permanently hide/remove the "Receiving valuated Stock in Tr" from the row display. Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Navigating Product Lifecycle Management in SAP IBP for Demand
entry.content: Thanks Laura. Turns out it was user error on my end. Thanks for the blog post and reply.

entry.title: Re: Publication in Error on property-value pair SI_ENDTIM...
entry.content:
Okay I've finally got a SAP S-user ID account so I could look at the KB article that was linked in the below post:

2343089 - How to change the SI_ENDTIME property of an InfoObject - SAP for Me

This solution is the better solution if you don't want to change all your publications, and I've tested it, and it worked for me.  The text of the KB article is the following:

Symptom

How do I change the SI_ENDTIME property of an InfoObject using the SDK.

Environment

  • SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform 4.x

    Resolution

    To run this sample:

    1. Copy the attached sample jsp into the AdminTools folder of your Enterprise server (e.g. C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\tomcat\webapps\AdminTools)
    2. Edit the jsp page and enter the correct logon credentials and server name for your Enterprise system

If needed, modify the Infostore query to only return back the desired reports.

e.g.
To only update a single report with SI_ID 12345
boInfoObjects = (IInfoObjects)infoStore.query("Select * FROM CI_INFOOBJECTS where SI_ID=12345 And SI_ID > " + max_id +  " ORDER BY SI_ID ASC");

To modify all instances of a report with SI_ID 12345
boInfoObjects = (IInfoObjects)infoStore.query("Select * FROM CI_INFOOBJECTS where SI_INSTANCE=1 AND SI_PARENTID=12345 And SI_ID > " + max_id +  " ORDER BY SI_ID ASC");

Run the sample by opening a browser window and going to http://myServer:8080/AdminTools/changeEndTime.jsp

Keywords

SI_ENDTIME, infoobject query error modify

The file that the above guide references is called changeEndTime.JSP.  Note - make sure you change the date in the file below to be further out in time based on your needs.  I set mine to 2040.  If we're still using this version of BOE in 16 years I'd be surprised!!

The content of that file is:

<%@ page import = "com.crystaldecisions.sdk.exception.SDKException,
                   com.crystaldecisions.sdk.framework.*,
                   com.crystaldecisions.sdk.occa.infostore.*,
				   com.crystaldecisions.sdk.properties.*,
                   java.util.*"
%><%

// User Credentials
String username = "Administrator";
String password = "Password1";
String cmsname  = "localhost";
String authType = "secEnterprise";

IEnterpriseSession enterpriseSession  = null;
IInfoStore infoStore;
IInfoObjects boInfoObjects; 
  
enterpriseSession = CrystalEnterprise.getSessionMgr().logon(username, password, cmsname, authType);
infoStore = (IInfoStore)enterpriseSession.getService("", "InfoStore");

int max_id = 0;

	for(;;) { 
		// Loop through all webi reports on the system
        boInfoObjects = (IInfoObjects)infoStore.query("Select * FROM CI_INFOOBJECTS where SI_ID=5651 And SI_ID > " + max_id +  " ORDER BY SI_ID ASC");
                                               
        if(boInfoObjects.size() == 0) 
            break;

		for(Iterator boCount = boInfoObjects.iterator() ; boCount.hasNext() ; ) {
            IInfoObject boReport1 = (IInfoObject)boCount.next();
            
            out.println("Checking Report Instance: " + boReport1.getID() + "<BR>");			
			IProperties tmpProps = boReport1.getSchedulingInfo().properties();
			tmpProps.setProperty("SI_ENDTIME", new java.util.Date("7/13/26 4:15:00 PM"));
			// 2026-09-01 12:00:00

            max_id = boReport1.getID();
        }
		infoStore.commit(boInfoObjects);            
	}
	out.println("Job Completed");
%>

entry.title: Comparative Analysis of SAP PaPM: Standard Model vs. Universal Model. Nutshell.
entry.content:
Hello dear colleagues, thank you for your attention to my first topic here!

Today, I would like to present my perspective on the differences between the SAP PaPM Standard Model and Universal Model based on my previous experience.

Let’s get started! 


0. Interface
 
The Standard Model interface:

 

The Universal Model interface:

 

In my opinion, the Universal Model has a more convenient and flexible interface. From the main screen, we can deep - dive into any function, model entity, or other element, regardless of the environment. In the Standard Model, however, we must enter a specific model to view these elements.

 

1. Data Structure and Management

Standard Model

The Standard Model is based on predefined structures and dimensions, which means data handling here is typically straightforward. It comes with SAP’s native, built in data structures that are already well aligned with ERP data and other standard SAP data models. This makes data mapping and management easier, especially for businesses that are using SAP ERP, S/4HANA, or other SAP solutions.

  • Advantages: Lower complexity, as data is often seamlessly compatible with existing SAP systems. You can plug in data from SAP modules (like Controlling, Financials, Sales, and Distribution) without complex transformation processes.

  • Limitations: It’s less flexible when integrating non - SAP data sources or when complex data transformation is needed. The predefined structures limit you to fixed hierarchies and data flow. For example, if you have customer segmentation data in a non - SAP CRM or need to process massive data sets from a cloud source, the Standard Model may not handle this as smoothly.

Universal Model

The Universal Model is far more flexible with data structure and management. It’s built to pull from various sources, whether they’re SAP or non - SAP, relational databases, data lakes, or even Excel files. It supports advanced transformations, letting you modify and enrich the data as it flows into SAP PaPM.

  • Advantages: Offers full flexibility to model and transform data as needed, regardless of source. You can add data sources or dimensions on - the - fly, such as customer feedback data from a CRM, social media engagement metrics, or environmental data for carbon footprint tracking.

  • Example Use Case: A utility company using SAP PaPM Universal Model could pull weather data from an external API to adjust profitability analysis based on seasonal demand. This level of integration would be difficult in the Standard Model, but the Universal Model’s flexibility makes it achievable.

 

2. Calculation Flexibility and Custom Logic

Standard Model

The Standard Model follows SAP’s standard logic and functions for profitability calculations, focusing on core, traditional metrics. Think of it as a structured calculation engine: you input data, and the model handles cost allocation, revenue segmentation, and basic profitability metrics with prebuilt functions.

  • Advantages: Simplifies the setup process because the core logic is already there, and all you need to do is configure it. For straightforward calculations like splitting revenue across regions or allocating shared service costs based on usage this works well.

  • Limitations: If you have complex logic that requires multi layered calculations or non - standard rules, the Standard Model can be limiting. For example, it may not be able to handle a scenario where costs need to be allocated based on advanced drivers, such as machine learning predictions on customer profitability.

Universal Model

The Universal Model supports entirely customizable logic and complex calculations. This means you can create custom rules and formulas to allocate costs and revenues exactly as needed. Whether you’re setting up multi - step allocations, recursive calculations, or layering calculations across different dimensions, the Universal Model can handle it.

  • Advanced Calculation Examples:
    • A manufacturing company could build multi step allocation rules for calculating end - to - end product costs, factoring in every phase of production, logistics, and marketing. Each stage could have its own set of complex drivers, like machine uptime, resource consumption, and location specific logistics costs.
    • A bank using the Universal Model could define profitability calculations for various customer risk segments, integrating variables like credit score, transaction volume, and regulatory compliance costs.

 

3. Performance and Scalability

Standard Model

With its predefined structure, the Standard Model is optimized for performance with typical SAP data volumes. It’s fast and efficient for calculations on small to medium datasets, and since the processing logic is preconfigured, it’s often resource efficient.

  • Best Fit: For businesses that don’t need to process massive volumes of data or carry out real - time updates. If you have, say, monthly profitability reports, and your data comes from a predictable set of sources, the Standard Model will be able to handle this with ease.

Universal Model

The Universal Model, being more customizable, is also more resource intensive. It’s built to handle larger datasets, complex data flows, and custom calculations, but this requires more computational power and potentially a more complex technical setup.

  • Scalability: Suited for businesses that are processing high volumes of transactions or need real - time analytics. The Universal Model is designed to process extensive data, so it’s capable of handling intricate, time - sensitive analyses.

  • Use Case: A global telecom company might need daily profitability reports across thousands of products, services, and regions, with real - time data on customer churn and network usage. This type of high - frequency, high - volume analysis is well suited to the Universal Model’s capabilities

 

4. Implementation Time and Effort

Standard Model

The predefined structure of the Standard Model means that it’s typically quicker to deploy and requires fewer resources. Implementing it involves configuring existing templates rather than building custom ones, which can reduce both cost and time to go - live.

  • Best Choice: Companies with tight timelines and simpler requirements, like a small - to - mid - sized retail chain looking to track store - level profitability. This setup could be ready in weeks with minimal customization.

Universal Model

The Universal Model’s flexibility requires more design and planning. Each custom dimension, rule, or source integration can extend the project timeline. Additionally, testing complex logic and integrating multiple data sources requires a deeper understanding of both the business logic and the technical setup.

  • Implementation Timeline: Companies opting for the Universal Model should expect implementation times ranging from several months to over a year for very complex setups. For instance, a bank with multiple profitability models across various regions, each with unique regulatory and financial structures, might take up to a year to go live with a fully customized Universal Model.

 

5. Who Benefits Most from Each Model?

Standard Model

This model is ideal for companies with relatively straightforward profitability and performance management needs, and where the business isn’t likely to change dimensions or structures frequently.

Universal Model

This model is designed for businesses that need deep customization and scalability. If your organization requires regular updates to its profitability models, or if it needs to integrate new data sources regularly, the Universal Model is a better choice.

 

 6. Example content 

Currently, most content is available for the Standard Model, likely because it was introduced much earlier than the universal model. You can explore all content for both the universal and standard models through the link below.

Example content PaPM Standard/Universal models 

Also, please take a look at this link, where you can find future updates and other related topics.

PaPM roadmap 


7. Conclusion

The Standard Model is a correct choice if you’re after rapid implementation, reliable performance with basic data, and don’t require custom calculations or complex data integrations. It’s a time and cost efficient option for many businesses with standard profitability, costing and allocations needs.

The Universal Model is perfect for enterprises that need more control and customization. While it requires a heavier initial investment of time and resources, it allows for advanced calculations, multi - source data integration, and deep customization that can drive complex profitability insights.

Ultimately, the choice between these models depends on your organization’s specific needs for data flexibility, complexity, and scalability in profitability management. If in doubt, some companies may start with the Standard Model for a quick win and then transition to the Universal Model as their analytical needs grow.

  
           Feature                        Standard Model                                      Universal Model
  Data StructurePredefined dimensions and hierarchies; suited for typical SAP ERP data.Customizable dimensions and hierarchies; supports SAP and non-SAP sources.
    Calculation Standardized, straightforward allocation and profitability formulas.Fully customizable; supports complex, multi-layered calculations.
 Data IntegrationPrimarily integrates with SAP data sources.Supports integration with SAP, non-SAP sources, data lakes, and external data.
  PerformanceOptimized for smaller to medium data sets; fast for simple calculations.Scalable for large, complex datasets; suited for high-volume, real - time analytics.
 Implementation     TimeFaster deployment (weeks), as templates and standard logic are prebuilt.Longer deployment (months to a year) due to customization and complex data integration.

 

Thank you for taking the time to read this article. If you notice any mistakes or inaccuracies, please feel free to point them out in the comments. Let’s collaborate to improve this solution. I also invite you to follow me, as I will soon be sharing in details my experience implementing ABC Costing for a mining company. Additionally, I will later explore the Sustainability topic, where I also have project experience.

Let's stay in touch - connect with me on Linkedin

entry.title: 50% self life materials to be not considered for sales but it can be used for production
entry.content:
I have spare parts sold and used as Bom components for production.

each material have self life

if the self life got expired 50% then it should not be considered for sales. New receipts to be created in Mrp. But for production the 50% expired material can be used and considered for Mrp

Is there any standard way to achieve this

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new Adaptation Editor experience for SAPUI5 adaptation projects
entry.content:
Hi Oliver, thanks for your answer. I just want to share that we update in a different way:

In BAS, we went to the "Open application information" option, click on it and it will open a window where you will be able to update the project.

At the end your project will be displayed as updated.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: S4TWL-RFQ Simplified Transaction - standard transactions support end date
Re: Still unable to pass DateTime parameters with "MS OLE...
entry.content: This time I tested the issue with SQL Server 2022 and MS OLE DB Driver 19 for SQL Server (latest version 19.3.5).

entry.title: Re: Still unable to pass DateTime parameters with "MS OLE...
entry.content: Last time it was most likey with SQL Server 2019. Same outcome.

entry.title: Re: Business Role Groups & Business User Groups
entry.content: I'm really not sure how this qualifies as a 'Solution'. Sure, you can add Business Roles to a Business Role Group. And then what? You cannot assign that role group to a User. You can add Business Users to a Business User Group. And then what? You cannot assign roles to that User Group. Furthermore, you cannot assign Role Groups to User Groups, so like the OP said, I'm not sure what purpose these functions actually serve. The statement that this 'solution' provides ease of maintenance makes no sense if you can't maintain anything apart from the group membership. If the purpose was just to add a 'tag' to the user for ease if identification, then just add a field to the user record. I sense that there is probably a roadmap for these functions and it just hasn't arrived yet.

entry.title: Re: Is there any Park JV & Invoices Option Available in S...
entry.content:
Hi

I see there are apps to support parking supplier invoices, like Park Incoming Invoice, Create Supplier Invoice.

But for parking general journal entries, following SAP note 2758922 explains How to park general journal entries with app "Verify General Journal Entries".

2758922 - How to park general journal entries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition environment - SAP for Me

please also check the Help Portal documentation on how to verify general journal entries,

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/6b33e9e760b840098d4c66f49121dc98.html

Customer invoice park function is not available in S/4HANA Cloud. You may submit a request at Influence portal for this functionality.

https://influence.sap.com/

Kind Regards

Lucy

entry.title: Re: OpenText VIM Invoice approval app not displaying DP number
entry.content: Hi Jeetendar, Try filling out the following fields: List Display, Length Detai,l Sequence Detail, Display Length. Use a number is not already being used for List and Detail sequence.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | 2023 FPS02 Release – Part 1
entry.content:
Hi Christian,

Thanks for the summary. Re one of the new features mentioned under Transportation Management on the ability to allow pick up and delivery stages to have different modes of transport, I am unclear whether this is available even before FSP02. Reason why I ask is when I looked at the information on SAP Help for example for the 2021 version (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e3dc5400c1cc41d1bc0ae0e7fd9aa5a2/06256f55b63e4e3aba39374122e2a0de.html?version=2021), the description of the feature is the same as 2023 latest version. Appreciate if you can clarify that as I am looking at using this feature and we are currently on 2022. Cheers.

 

Regards

SF

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services and SAP Information Steward
entry.content: Is SAP Data Services 4.3 SP02 compatible with SuSE15 SP5

entry.title: Re: SUPPLIER INVOICE
entry.content:
Hi,

You can see the created by information by display the Display Document Header option when you display the individual documents.

Hope this helps.

Kind Regards,

Lucy

entry.title: Re: CMC Sessions
entry.content:
At least for us (SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 4.3 Support Pack 3 Patch 3 Version: 14.3.3.4562), I believe the maximum number of sessions a user can have in CMC or OpenDocument is ten within 20 minutes. Apache Tomcat sets the 20 minutes, so adjusting this will affect the entire system, not just OpenDocument.

In SAP BusinessObjects, the OpenDocument feature utilizes the platform's session management to handle user authentication and document access. By default, user sessions in BusinessObjects are configured to expire after 20 minutes of inactivity. This timeout setting is consistent across various components, including OpenDocument.

Session Timeout Configuration:

  • Default Timeout: 20 minutes of inactivity.
  • Configuration File: The session timeout can be adjusted in the web.xml file of the respective web application.
  • File Location: Typically found at C:\Program Files\Business Objects\Tomcat55\webapps\InfoViewApp\WEB-INF\web.xml.

We haven't figured out a way to extend max sessions, as we don't want to change the 20-minute timeout session default.

entry.title: 9/80 Work Schedule
entry.content: How are other companies practicing the 9/80 work schedule when it comes to taking time off in SAP?

Kind Regards,

Lucy

Soma

entry.title: Addition of a custom Key attribute to Supply Planning levels to enable TS Supply Planning
entry.content:
Hello IBP Guru's,

Is it possible to add a custom key attribute to all the planning levels related to input/output supply planning key figures for e.g. WKPRODCUST and WKPROLOCCUST? If so what is the option to do so and enable TS supply planning at this key custom attribute?

Thanks,

Mangesh

entry.title: Re: MESSAGE Unable to resolve plug-in "com.sap.adt.ddic.t...
entry.content:
what you did in that popup?

did you select all and trust them?

entry.title: Level Based Hierarchy description not working on lower level
entry.content:
Hello community,

I am currently working with a Custom Customer hierarchy, and I am facing a strange thing when using it.

I have built a level-based hierarchy dimension, and I am using it via an association with my fact view.

When I am previewing the data in the analytic model or in SAC I have a strange bug. When using the Customer dimension without the hierarchy (flat presentation) I have the customer ID and Description working fine. 

If I select the hierarchy, I now loose the description for the customer but it's still working for the other level of the hierarchy. Only the description from the last level is lost, so in my case, I loose the customer description. It now show the ID in the description.

Do any of you have seen something like that?

Thanks

entry.title: Career Guide
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

This is Goku, and I am a Supply Chain and Operations management student. I have over year of internship experience working as Production Coordinator in a Aerospace Industry. I have learning Business process and little bit of end user experience in SAP Production Planning. Currently I am 3rd year of my college and I am learing advanced business process in SAP. As of now, I found out that I am interested in SAP and consulting sounds interesting to me what I have researched so far. I need help with finding good course or how can I start my career in SAP functional consulting? Please if you have any hints or guidance would be helpful. I googled how to become a SAP Functional consutant, and most the finding I got was need degree in IT and related field. 

It would be helpful if someone provide with certification information or how to become a SAP consultant? 

I found the course below in SAP certification website but I am not sure if it will be useful or not? Please guide! 

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-business-process-integration-with-sap-s-4hana

entry.title: Re: People Analitycs - Issue with loading story reports
entry.content: Hi, Is this resolved? How did you managed that?

entry.title: Career Guidance!
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

This is Goku, and I am a Supply Chain and Operations management student. I have over year of internship experience working as Production Coordinator in a Aerospace Industry. I have learning Business process and little bit of end user experience in SAP Production Planning. Currently I am 3rd year of my college and I am learing advanced business process in SAP. As of now, I found out that I am interested in SAP and consulting sounds interesting to me what I have researched so far. I need help with finding good course or how can I start my career in SAP functional consulting? Please if you have any hints or guidance would be helpful. I googled how to become a SAP Functional consutant, and most the finding I got was need degree in IT and related field. 

It would be helpful if someone provide with certification information or how to become a SAP consultant? 

I found the course below in SAP certification website but I am not sure if it will be useful or not? Please guide! 

https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-business-process-integration-with-sap-s-4hana
